text.div:
Generated Narrative: StructureDefinition ems-medicationadministration

NameFlagsCard.TypeDescription & Constraints
MedicationAdministration 0..*MedicationAdministrationAdministration of medication to a patient
Slices for extension 0..*ExtensioneMedications.02/07/08/11/13 - EMS Medication Context
Slice: Unordered, Open by value:url
extension:medicationContext S0..1(Complex)EMS Medication Administration Context
URL: https://fhirems.github.io/fhirEMSCoreIG/StructureDefinition/ext-ems-medication-context
status S1..1codein-progress | not-done | on-hold | completed | entered-in-error | stopped | unknown
medication[x] S1..1CodeableConceptWhat was administered
coding S0..*CodingCode defined by a terminology system
subject S1..1Reference(EMS Patient)Who received medication
context S0..1Reference(EMS Encounter)The EMS encounter during which this medication was administered
effective[x] S1..1dateTimeeMedications.01 - Date/Time Medication Administered
performer S0..*BackboneElementWho performed the medication administration and what they did
function S0..1CodeableConcepteMedications.10 - Role/Type of Person Administering Medication
Binding: EMS Caregiver Level (required)
actor 1..1Reference(EMS Practitioner | EMS PractitionerRole)eMedications.09 - Medication Crew (Healthcare Professionals) ID
dosage S0..1BackboneElementDetails of how medication was taken
route S0..1CodeableConcepteMedications.04 - Medication Administered Route
Binding: EMS Medication Administered Route (required)
dose S0..1SimpleQuantity(4.0.1)eMedications.05 - Medication Dosage
unit S0..1stringUnit representation

Documentation for this format

name: EMSMedicationAdministration
title: EMS Medication Administration
description:
EMS medication administration derived directly from FHIR R4
MedicationAdministration. No US Core profile exists for this resource
(NDR-008). Represents the NEMSIS eMedications section
(Version 3.5.1.251001CP2).

NEMSIS eMedications records what was ACTUALLY administered in the field,
not what was ordered. Always use MedicationAdministration, never
MedicationRequest, for NEMSIS eMedications data.

eMedications.03 uses RxNorm codes (direct pass-through from NEMSIS).
Additional NEMSIS context (prior EMS care, response, complication,
authorization, by-another-unit) is carried in EMSMedicationContext.

element.short: Administration of medication to a patient
element.definition: Describes the event of a patient consuming or otherwise being administered a medication.  This may be as simple as swallowing a tablet or it may be a long running infusion.  Related resources tie this event to the authorizing prescription, and the specific encounter between patient and health care practitioner.

constraint.human: If the resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT contain nested Resources
If the resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL be referred to from elsewhere in the resource or SHALL refer to the containing resource
constraint.expression: contained.where((('#'+id in (%resource.descendants().reference | %resource.descendants().as(canonical) | %resource.descendants().as(uri) | %resource.descendants().as(url))) or descendants().where(reference = '#').exists() or descendants().where(as(canonical) = '#').exists() or descendants().where(as(canonical) = '#').exists()).not()).trace('unmatched', id).empty()
constraint.xpath: not(exists(for $id in f:contained/*/f:id/@value return $contained[not(parent::*/descendant::f:reference/@value=concat('#', $contained/*/id/@value) or descendant::f:reference[@value='#'])]))

constraint.human: If a resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT have a meta.versionId or a meta.lastUpdated
If a resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT have a security label

extension.valueMarkdown: When a resource has no narrative, only systems that fully understand the data can display the resource to a human safely. Including a human readable representation in the resource makes for a much more robust eco-system and cheaper handling of resources by intermediary systems. Some ecosystems restrict distribution of resources to only those systems that do fully understand the resources, and as a consequence implementers may believe that the narrative is superfluous. However experience shows that such eco-systems often open up to new participants over time.

constraint.human: A resource should have narrative for robust management

element.definition: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.
element.comment: The only time that a resource does not have an id is when it is being submitted to the server using a create operation.

element.definition: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: A set of rules under which this content was created
element.definition: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.
element.comment: Asserting this rule set restricts the content to be only understood by a limited set of trading partners. This inherently limits the usefulness of the data in the long term. However, the existing health eco-system is highly fractured, and not yet ready to define, collect, and exchange data in a generally computable sense. Wherever possible, implementers and/or specification writers should avoid using this element. Often, when used, the URL is a reference to an implementation guide that defines these special rules as part of it's narrative along with other profiles, value sets, etc.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This element is labeled as a modifier because the implicit rules may provide additional knowledge about the resource that modifies it's meaning or interpretation

element.definition: The base language in which the resource is written.
element.comment: Language is provided to support indexing and accessibility (typically, services such as text to speech use the language tag). The html language tag in the narrative applies  to the narrative. The language tag on the resource may be used to specify the language of other presentations generated from the data in the resource. Not all the content has to be in the base language. The Resource.language should not be assumed to apply to the narrative automatically. If a language is specified, it should it also be specified on the div element in the html (see rules in HTML5 for information about the relationship between xml:lang and the html lang attribute).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Text summary of the resource, for human interpretation
element.definition: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.
element.comment: Contained resources do not have narrative. Resources that are not contained SHOULD have a narrative. In some cases, a resource may only have text with little or no additional discrete data (as long as all minOccurs=1 elements are satisfied).  This may be necessary for data from legacy systems where information is captured as a "text blob" or where text is additionally entered raw or narrated and encoded information is added later.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.
element.comment: This should never be done when the content can be identified properly, as once identification is lost, it is extremely difficult (and context dependent) to restore it again. Contained resources may have profiles and tags In their meta elements, but SHALL NOT have security labels.

element.definition: An Extension
element.comment: Carries NEMSIS eMedications elements not representable in base FHIR MedicationAdministration: priorEMSCare (.02), response (.07), complication (.08), authorization (.11), byAnotherUnit (.13).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.definition: Contextual data for NEMSIS eMedications not directly representable in FHIR MedicationAdministration: prior EMS care indicator, clinical response, complication(s), and authorization type.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.
element.requirements: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored.  This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.isModifierReason: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the resource that contains them

element.definition: Identifiers associated with this Medication Administration that are defined by business processes and/or used to refer to it when a direct URL reference to the resource itself is not appropriate. They are business identifiers assigned to this resource by the performer or other systems and remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.
element.comment: This is a business identifier, not a resource identifier.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: A protocol, guideline, orderset, or other definition that was adhered to in whole or in part by this event.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: A larger event of which this particular event is a component or step.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: in-progress | not-done | on-hold | completed | entered-in-error | stopped | unknown
element.definition: Will generally be set to show that the administration has been completed.  For some long running administrations such as infusions, it is possible for an administration to be started but not completed or it may be paused while some other process is under way.
element.comment: Use 'completed' for administered medications. Use 'not-done' for NV (NotRecorded → extend with dataAbsentReason pattern).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This element is labelled as a modifier because it is a status element that contains status entered-in-error which means that the resource should not be treated as valid

binding.description: A set of codes indicating the current status of a MedicationAdministration.

element.definition: A code indicating why the administration was not performed.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: A set of codes indicating the reason why the MedicationAdministration is negated.

mapping.map: RXA-9-Administration Notes / RXA-18 Substance/Treatment Refusal Reason

element.definition: Indicates where the medication is expected to be consumed or administered.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: A coded concept describing where the medication administered is expected to occur.

element.definition: Identifies the medication that was administered. This is either a link to a resource representing the details of the medication or a simple attribute carrying a code that identifies the medication from a known list of medications.
element.comment: If only a code is specified, then it needs to be a code for a specific product. If more information is required, then the use of the medication resource is recommended.  For example, if you require form or lot number, then you must reference the Medication resource.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: Codes identifying substance or product that can be administered.

element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Code defined by a terminology system
element.definition: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.
element.comment: NEMSIS eMedications.03. Usage: Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable. PN: ExamFindingNotPresent, UnableToComplete. NEMSIS stores RxNorm codes directly — use system http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm.
element.requirements: Allows for alternative encodings within a code system, and translations to other code systems.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Plain text representation of the concept
element.definition: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.
element.comment: Very often the text is the same as a displayName of one of the codings.
element.requirements: The codes from the terminologies do not always capture the correct meaning with all the nuances of the human using them, or sometimes there is no appropriate code at all. In these cases, the text is used to capture the full meaning of the source.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: C*E.9. But note many systems use C*E.2 for this

element.definition: The person or animal or group receiving the medication.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: The EMS encounter during which this medication was administered
element.definition: The visit, admission, or other contact between patient and health care provider during which the medication administration was performed.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: .inboundRelationship[typeCode=COMP].source[classCode=ENC, moodCode=EVN, code="type of encounter or episode"]

element.definition: Additional information (for example, patient height and weight) that supports the administration of the medication.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: .outboundRelationship[typeCode=PERT].target[A_SupportingClinicalStatement CMET minimal with many different choices of classCodes(ORG, ENC, PROC, SPLY, SBADM, OBS) and each of the act class codes draws from one or more of the following moodCodes (EVN, DEF, INT PRMS, RQO, PRP, APT, ARQ, GOL)]

element.definition: A specific date/time or interval of time during which the administration took place (or did not take place, when the 'notGiven' attribute is true). For many administrations, such as swallowing a tablet the use of dateTime is more appropriate.
element.comment: NEMSIS eMedications.01. Usage: Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: RXA-3-Date/Time Start of Administration / RXA-4-Date/Time End of Administration

element.short: Who performed the medication administration and what they did
element.definition: Indicates who or what performed the medication administration and how they were involved.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: RXA-10-Administering Provider / PRT-5-Participation Person: PRT-4-Participation='AP' (RXA-10 is deprecated)

Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.isModifierReason: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

element.short: eMedications.10 - Role/Type of Person Administering Medication
element.definition: Distinguishes the type of involvement of the performer in the medication administration.
element.comment: NEMSIS eMedications.10. Usage: Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Indicates who or what performed the medication administration.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: A code indicating why the medication was given.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: A set of codes indicating the reason why the MedicationAdministration was made.

element.short: Condition or observation that supports why the medication was administered
element.definition: Condition or observation that supports why the medication was administered.
element.comment: This is a reference to a condition that is the reason for the medication request.  If only a code exists, use reasonCode.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: The original request, instruction or authority to perform the administration.
element.comment: This is a reference to the MedicationRequest  where the intent is either order or instance-order.  It should not reference MedicationRequests where the intent is any other value.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: ORC-2-Placer Order Number / ORC-3-Filler Order Number

element.definition: The device used in administering the medication to the patient.  For example, a particular infusion pump.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: PRT-10 Participation Device / RXR-3 Administrative Device

element.definition: Extra information about the medication administration that is not conveyed by the other attributes.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Details of how medication was taken
element.definition: Describes the medication dosage information details e.g. dose, rate, site, route, etc.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
SHALL have at least one of dosage.dose or dosage.rate[x]

element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.isModifierReason: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

element.short: Free text dosage instructions e.g. SIG
element.definition: Free text dosage can be used for cases where the dosage administered is too complex to code. When coded dosage is present, the free text dosage may still be present for display to humans.The dosage instructions should reflect the dosage of the medication that was administered.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: A coded specification of the anatomic site where the medication first entered the body.  For example, "left arm".
element.comment: If the use case requires attributes from the BodySite resource (e.g. to identify and track separately) then use the standard extension [bodySite](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extension-bodysite.html).  May be a summary code, or a reference to a very precise definition of the location, or both.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: A coded concept describing the site location the medicine enters into or onto the body.

mapping.map: RXR-2 Administration Site / RXR-6 Administration Site Modifier

element.definition: A code specifying the route or physiological path of administration of a therapeutic agent into or onto the patient.  For example, topical, intravenous, etc.
element.comment: NEMSIS eMedications.04. Usage: Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable. PN: ExamFindingNotPresent, UnableToComplete.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: A coded value indicating the method by which the medication is intended to be or was introduced into or on the body.  This attribute will most often NOT be populated.  It is most commonly used for injections.  For example, Slow Push, Deep IV.
element.comment: One of the reasons this attribute is not used often, is that the method is often pre-coordinated with the route and/or form of administration.  This means the codes used in route or form may pre-coordinate the method in the route code or the form code.  The implementation decision about what coding system to use for route or form code will determine how frequently the method code will be populated e.g. if route or form code pre-coordinate method code, then this attribute will not be populated often; if there is no pre-coordination then method code may  be used frequently.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: A coded concept describing the technique by which the medicine is administered.

element.definition: The amount of the medication given at one administration event.   Use this value when the administration is essentially an instantaneous event such as a swallowing a tablet or giving an injection.
element.comment: NEMSIS eMedications.05 (numeric dose amount). Usage: Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: RXA-6 Administered Amount / RXA-7 Administered Units

element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.definition: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.
element.comment: The implicit precision in the value should always be honored. Monetary values have their own rules for handling precision (refer to standard accounting text books).
element.requirements: Precision is handled implicitly in almost all cases of measurement.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: PQ.value, CO.value, MO.value, IVL.high or IVL.low depending on the value

element.definition: Not allowed to be used in this context
element.requirements: Need a framework for handling measures where the value is <5ug/L or >400mg/L due to the limitations of measuring methodology.
element.meaningWhenMissing: If there is no comparator, then there is no modification of the value

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This is labeled as "Is Modifier" because the comparator modifies the interpretation of the value significantly. If there is no comparator, then there is no modification of the value

binding.description: How the Quantity should be understood and represented.

element.definition: A human-readable form of the unit.
element.comment: NEMSIS eMedications.06 - Medication Dosage Units. Usage: Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable. Code from EMSMedicationDosageUnitsVS.
element.requirements: There are many representations for units of measure and in many contexts, particular representations are fixed and required. I.e. mcg for micrograms.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: System that defines coded unit form
element.definition: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.
element.requirements: Need to know the system that defines the coded form of the unit.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.
element.comment: The preferred system is UCUM, but SNOMED CT can also be used (for customary units) or ISO 4217 for currency.  The context of use may additionally require a code from a particular system.
element.requirements: Need a computable form of the unit that is fixed across all forms. UCUM provides this for quantities, but SNOMED CT provides many units of interest.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Dose quantity per unit of time
element.definition: Identifies the speed with which the medication was or will be introduced into the patient.  Typically, the rate for an infusion e.g. 100 ml per 1 hour or 100 ml/hr.  May also be expressed as a rate per unit of time, e.g. 500 ml per 2 hours.  Other examples:  200 mcg/min or 200 mcg/1 minute; 1 liter/8 hours.
element.comment: If the rate changes over time, and you want to capture this in MedicationAdministration, then each change should be captured as a distinct MedicationAdministration, with a specific MedicationAdministration.dosage.rate, and the date time when the rate change occurred. Typically, the MedicationAdministration.dosage.rate element is not used to convey an average rate.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: A list of events of interest in the lifecycle
element.definition: A summary of the events of interest that have occurred, such as when the administration was verified.
element.comment: This might not include provenances for all versions of the request – only those deemed “relevant” or important. This SHALL NOT include the Provenance associated with this current version of the resource. (If that provenance is deemed to be a “relevant” change, it will need to be added as part of a later update. Until then, it can be queried directly as the Provenance that points to this version using _revinclude All Provenances should have some historical version of this Request as their subject.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.comment: Carries NEMSIS eMedications elements not representable in base FHIR MedicationAdministration: priorEMSCare (.02), response (.07), complication (.08), authorization (.11), byAnotherUnit (.13).
Use 'completed' for administered medications. Use 'not-done' for NV (NotRecorded → extend with dataAbsentReason pattern).

NEMSIS eMedications.03. Usage: Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable. PN: ExamFindingNotPresent, UnableToComplete. NEMSIS stores RxNorm codes directly — use system http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm.

element.short: The EMS encounter during which this medication was administered

element.comment: NEMSIS eMedications.01. Usage: Required/National. Nillable. NV: NotRecorded, NotApplicable.
